name: docs-write
description: Write documentation following Metabase's conversational, clear, and user-focused style. Use when creating or editing documentation files (markdown, MDX, etc.).
capabilities: Create markdown documentation files following Metabase style
Edit existing documentation with consistent voice and tone
Format instructions with clear action steps
Apply American spelling and serial commas
Run prettier for consistent documentation formatting
author: metabase
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill containing only markdown writing guidelines. No executable code exists. Static findings are false positives: backticks in markdown examples were misinterpreted as shell execution, angle brackets in placeholder syntax were flagged as cryptographic issues, and scanner hallucinated Windows SAM database references that do not exist. The skill only provides documentation writing instructions.
valueStatement: Documentation often frustrates readers with jargon and unnecessary complexity. This skill provides clear guidelines for writing conversational, user-focused documentation that helps people find answers fast.
limitations: Does not generate code examples (only documentation about code)
Does not handle translations or localization

useCases.title: Create documentation guides
useCases.description: Write new markdown documentation following established style patterns and voice guidelines.

useCases.title: Document features
useCases.description: Add or update feature documentation with clear instructions and examples.

useCases.title: Apply consistent style
useCases.description: Edit any documentation file to match Metabase conversational style standards.

promptTemplates.title: Write new docs
promptTemplates.prompt: Write documentation for [feature/topic] in markdown. Include clear steps, practical examples, and links to related content.

promptTemplates.title: Edit existing docs
promptTemplates.prompt: Review and edit this documentation for clarity and style. Lead with actions, cut filler, use descriptive headings.

promptTemplates.title: Format instructions
promptTemplates.prompt: Write step-by-step instructions for [task]. Use code blocks for commands, bold for UI elements, and explain context after steps.

promptTemplates.title: Apply style guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply Metabase style guidelines to this documentation: conversational tone, action-first steps, descriptive links, no jargon.

bestPractices: Match complexity to your audience - do not oversimplify hard topics or overcomplicate simple ones
Lead with the ask, then explain why - put action before explanation
Use headings that state your point, not just the topic - write Set SAML before adding users not SAML configuration
antiPatterns: Using we when talking about product features - say Metabase or it instead
Using corporate jargon like utilize, reference, or offerings
Describing tasks as easy or simple - you do not know the readers context

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: Markdown (md), MDX, and other documentation file formats commonly used in documentation projects.

faq.question: Does this skill handle formatting?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes guidance to run prettier for consistent formatting after edits.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with other writing tools?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Use it alongside any editor or writing tool.

faq.question: Does this skill access or store my documentation?
faq.answer: The skill only accesses files you explicitly open or edit. No data is stored externally.

faq.question: What if my documentation uses a different style?
faq.answer: The skill applies Metabase style specifically. Adapt the guidelines for other style requirements as needed.

faq.question: How is this different from general writing skills?
faq.answer: This skill enforces specific patterns: action-first instructions, conversational tone, no jargon, descriptive links, and UI element formatting.
